# W29T3 Implementation Plan

## Task Goal

Implement the specialized operation test suite across the main integration-test files for math, broadcast, indexing, construction, iteration, reduction, matrix, set, shape, conversion, utility, output, FFI, workspace, parallel, and SIMD behavior. This task delivers the broad functional coverage promised by `design/28-tests.md`.

## Related Design Documents

- `design/28-tests.md` §8.2-§8.19
- `design/28-tests.md` §8.21-§8.23
- `design/28-tests.md` §9.1-§9.1a
- `design/28-tests.md` Appendix task list, Wave 2 T3-T9b and Wave 3 T11-T14
- `design/25-safety.md` §8.6-§8.9, for parallel safety checks
- `design/00-coding.md` §5.1-§5.4

## Dependencies

- W29T1

## Files to Create/Modify

- `tests/test_math.rs`
- `tests/test_broadcast.rs`
- `tests/test_index.rs`
- `tests/test_construction.rs`
- `tests/test_iterator.rs`
- `tests/test_reduction.rs`
- `tests/test_matrix.rs`

- `tests/test_simd.rs`

## Implementation Details

### Required test function inventory

Implement the exact named tests from `design/28-tests.md`:

- `test_math.rs`: `test_add_same_shape`, `test_add_scalar`, `test_sub_mul_div`, `test_neg`, `test_abs_signum`, `test_sin_sqrt_exp_ln_floor_ceil`, `test_complex_math`, `test_bool_not`, `test_compare_eq_ne`, `test_compare_lt_gt`, `test_square`, `test_integer_add_overflow_panics`, `test_integer_divide_by_zero_panics`, `test_integer_min_abs_panics`, `test_integer_min_div_neg_one_panics`, `test_integer_neg_min_panics`, `test_integer_square_overflow_panics`
- `test_broadcast.rs`: `test_broadcast_scalar`, `test_broadcast_row_col`, `test_broadcast_left_pad`, `test_broadcast_incompatible`, `test_broadcast_view_readonly`, `test_broadcast_zero_stride`
- `test_index.rs`: `test_multi_dim_index`, `test_index_out_of_bounds`, `test_slice_range`, `test_slice_subrange`, `test_slice_mut_broadcast_rejected`
- `test_construction.rs`: `test_zeros_ones_from_scalar`, `test_eye_identity`, `test_from_data_constructors`, `test_from_fixed_array`, `test_from_shape_vec_f_order_mapping`
- `test_iterator.rs`: `test_iter_elements`, `test_axis_iter`, `test_axis_iter_ix0_runtime_error`, `test_indexed_iter`
- `test_reduction.rs`: `test_sum_global`, `test_sum_axis`, `test_sum_keepdims`, `test_sum_empty`, `test_sum_nan`, `test_integer_sum_overflow`
- `test_matrix.rs`: `test_dot_product`, `test_dot_complex`, `test_dot_shape_mismatch`, `test_dot_empty`, `test_i32_dot_overflow_panics`, `test_i64_dot_overflow_panics`
- `test_set.rs`: `test_unique_order_unspecified`, `test_unique_integers`, `test_unique_nan_preserved`, `test_unique_signed_zero_equal`, `test_unique_complex`, `test_unique_non_contiguous`
- `test_shape.rs`: `test_transpose_2d`, `test_transpose_high_dim`
- `test_conversion.rs`: `test_cast_f32_to_f64`, `test_cast_f64_to_f32`, `test_cast_real_to_complex`, `test_cast_complex_to_real_zero_imag`, `test_cast_complex_to_real_nonzero_imag`, `test_bool_not_participating_in_cast`, `test_cast_nan_to_int`
- `test_utility.rs`: `test_fill_inplace`, `test_clip`, `test_clip_non_contiguous`, `test_to_contiguous`, `test_clip_invalid_parameters`, `test_fill_rejects_readonly_or_broadcast`
- `test_output.rs`: `test_display_small_tensor`, `test_display_truncated`, `test_debug_includes_metadata`, `test_output_complex`, `test_scalar_vs_zero_dim_formatting`
- `test_ffi.rs`: `test_as_ptr`, `test_as_mut_ptr`, `test_lda`, `test_is_blas_layout_compatible`, `test_export_roundtrip`, `test_export_mut_roundtrip`, `test_from_raw_parts_mut_reject_overlap`, `test_try_offset_of`, `test_export_alignment_preconditions`
- `test_workspace.rs`: `test_workspace_new_invalid_alignment`, `test_workspace_borrow_rules`, `test_workspace_split`, `test_workspace_ensure_capacity`, `test_workspace_assume_init_prefix`, `test_workspace_error_boundary_mapping`, `test_workspace_not_send_not_sync`
- `test_parallel.rs`: `test_sum_parallel_feature_consistency`, `test_par_add_consistency`, `test_par_dot_consistency`, `test_parallel_read`, `test_nested_parallel_falls_back_to_serial`
- `test_simd.rs`: `test_simd_add_consistency`, `test_simd_sum_consistency`, `test_simd_dot_consistency`, `test_simd_fallback_small`, `test_simd_complex_path`

### Assertion and comparison rules

- Tier 1 exact helpers for same-path arithmetic, reduction, indexing, construction, and iterator checks
- Tier 2 cross-path tolerance helpers for SIMD versus scalar and parallel versus serial consistency tests
- Tier 3 math tolerance helpers for `sin`, `sqrt`, `exp`, `ln`, `floor`, and `ceil`
- `assert_xenon_error!` for documented recoverable error paths
- `#[should_panic]` or `catch_unwind` for integer overflow and contract panic cases

### Boundary coverage requirements

Fold in the required edge cases from `design/28-tests.md` §8.21:

- empty tensors
- single-element tensors
- large tensors where relevant
- NaN, Inf, subnormal, and integer extremes
- complex extreme components
- non-contiguous transpose or slice views
- high-dimensional `Ix4` through `Ix6` plus `IxDyn` representative scenarios

## Test Plan

- Run each test target independently with `cargo test --test <name>`
- Run `cargo test --test test_parallel --features parallel`
- Run `cargo test --test test_simd --features simd`
- Re-run representative math, reduction, matrix, set, and FFI files after all fixtures and helpers are shared correctly

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Every specialized test file listed above exists and contains the named tests from `design/28-tests.md`
- [ ] Float, complex, cross-path, and math-function comparisons use the correct helper tier
- [ ] Parallel and SIMD tests validate observable public behavior, not private backend internals
- [ ] Workspace, FFI, and output edge cases match the documented test matrix
- [ ] Boundary scenarios for empty, NaN, non-contiguous, high-rank, and overflow cases are covered

## Development Workflow

1. Implement domain test files in dependency order, starting with math, broadcast, and construction.
2. Add iterator, reduction, matrix, set, and shape tests that reuse the common fixtures.
3. Add conversion, utility, output, FFI, and workspace coverage.
4. Finish with feature-gated `parallel` and `simd` test files using the cross-path helper rules.
5. Run each test target individually before attempting the full matrix.
